title: Tender Irish Dish
description: A traditional French recipe featuring tender Irish ingredients, perfect for a comforting meal.
instructions: Season the tenderloin with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Heat the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the tenderloin on all sides until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 15-20 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare.
While the tenderloin is cooking, sauté the sliced onion and minced garlic in the skillet until softened.
Add the sugar to the skillet and stir to combine.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the tenderloin rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve the sliced tenderloin with the caramelized onion and garlic mixture spooned over the top.
